hypotheses.title: Two-Phase Mechanoenhancer Activation Constitutes a Temporal Coincidence Gate

novelty.finding: NOVEL — no published two-phase temporal dependency model for mechanoenhancer activation
search_queries: "two-phase" mechanoenhancer activation Piezo1 YAP temporal sequence H3K27ac

mechanism.finding: MOSTLY PLAUSIBLE — BRD4 heterogeneous nucleation on H3K27ac confirmed (Mol Biol Cell 2024 PMC11238092). CaMKII→HDAC4 export confirmed (Backs 2006). HDAC5 requires HDAC4 hetero-oligomerization for CaMKII responsiveness (not acknowledged). EP300 'freeing' mechanism oversimplified.
concerns: HDAC5 not directly responsive to CaMKII — requires HDAC4 hetero-oligomer
EP300 freeing via HDAC4/5 export is oversimplified; class IIa HDACs repress via NCoR/SMRT/HDAC3
Cell-type translation from cardiomyocytes to lung fibroblasts not verified

logic.finding: PASSES — temporal dependency is logically sound based on BRD4 nucleation biochemistry

falsifiability.finding: PASSES — dCas9-p300 rescue experiment is well-designed and discriminating

triviality.finding: PASSES — non-trivial cross-domain connection bridging BRD4 condensation physics with mechanobiology

counter_evidence.finding: No strong counter-evidence. Piezo1 and YAP typically treated as parallel, not sequential. Cell-type translation concern.
search_queries: Piezo1 CaMKII HDAC4 HDAC5 export EP300 H3K27ac mechanoenhancer temporal

inferred_claims: H3K27ac from Phase 1 necessary for BRD4 condensate nucleation in mechanoenhancer context — WELL-SUPPORTED by independent BRD4 nucleation literature

hallucination_as_novelty.finding: LOW RISK — all bridge components exist independently; novelty is in the connection

claim_level_verification.finding: Core claims verified. PMID 23804765 (cited in QG evidence, not hypothesis) RETRACTED March 2023.
warnings: PMID 23804765 (nuclear CaMKII→H3S10ph) RETRACTED — cited in QG new_evidence, not in hypothesis itself
Zanconato 2018 is Nature Medicine, not Nature Cancer

hypotheses.survival_note: Strongest cycle 2 hypothesis. BRD4 heterogeneous nucleation literature provides independent mechanistic confirmation. Main concerns: cell-type translation, HDAC5 dependency on HDAC4, EP300 mechanism oversimplification.

hypotheses.title: Lamin A/C Concentration Sets the Cell-Intrinsic Stiffness-Sensing Threshold for Mechanoenhancer Activation

novelty.finding: SEVERE NOVELTY WOUND — core concept (lamin A/C tension → YAP threshold) published in 2024 Nature Communications (PMID 39578439). Swift 2013 Science established lamin A scaling with stiffness. Extension to mechanoenhancer H3K27ac is incremental.
search_queries: lamin A/C YAP nuclear localization threshold stiffness sensing cell heterogeneity
lamin A/C mechanoenhancer enhancer activation stiffness threshold published 2024 2025
prior_work_refs: Wang et al. 2024 Nature Communications PMID 39578439

mechanism.finding: PLAUSIBLE — all established components. Novel step (YAP threshold → mechanoenhancer H3K27ac) is weakest link.
concerns: Multiple other YAP regulators (LATS1/2, Hippo, cell-cell contact) not considered
'Rheostat' language overstates lamin A/C's role vs other YAP inputs

logic.finding: MINOR WOUND — transitive chain is valid but 'rheostat' language overstates single-variable control

falsifiability.finding: PASSES — scCUT&RUN + lamin A IF + EC50 shift experiments are well-designed

triviality.finding: APPROACHES TRIVIALITY — core concept is well-known to mechanobiology community. Discher lab has published extensively on lamin A scaling. Mechanoenhancer extension is the only non-obvious element.

counter_evidence.finding: Lamin A/C dispensable for mechanical repression of adipogenesis (PMID 34205295). Lamin A/C protects chromatin accessibility during mechanical loading (2025) — different mechanism than threshold setting. Context-dependence threatens generalizability.
search_queries: lamin A/C mechanoenhancer enhancer activation stiffness threshold published 2024 2025
lamin A/C cell-to-cell variability single cell YAP stiffness bimodal heterogeneous response enhancer

grounded_claims: Lamin A/C KO → increased nuclear deformability → enhanced mechanosensing
Lamin A/C expression scales with ECM stiffness (Swift 2013)
Cell-to-cell variability in nuclear YAP on uniform substrates (bimodal at 10 kPa)
inferred_claims: Lamin A/C variability → H3K27ac variability at mechanoenhancers in quantitatively predictable way

hallucination_as_novelty.finding: LOW RISK — but novelty is inflated. Core concept is extension of known work.

claim_level_verification.finding: All GROUNDED claims verified. No citation hallucinations.
verified_claims: Bimodal YAP on 10 kPa substrates (2024 Nat Comms)

hypotheses.survival_note: Survives as WOUNDED because the mechanoenhancer H3K27ac prediction is technically untested. However, this is an incremental extension of published work (2024 Nat Comms), not a novel cross-domain discovery. Strongest reason to kill: the core concept is already published.

hypotheses.title: Nuclear Actin Polymerization State Regulates MRTF-A Residence Time at CaRG-Box Mechanoenhancers
hypotheses.kill_reason: Fabricated protein property (RPEL-F-actin binding) + hallucination masquerading as novelty

novelty.finding: Appears novel — but novel because the mechanism is structurally impossible, not because it's unexplored.
search_queries: nuclear F-actin MRTF-A RPEL domain chromatin tether dwell time formin
novelty.note: Absence of prior work is because the mechanism contradicts structural biology

mechanism.finding: CRITICAL FAILURE — RPEL domain binds G-actin exclusively, NOT F-actin. Structural studies (EMBO J 2008 PMC2583105, Science Signaling 2011) show RPEL-actin assemblies are 'not related to those in F-actin' and RPEL-F-actin binding is 'unlikely.' The actual mechanism is OPPOSITE: nuclear F-actin depletes nuclear G-actin → G-actin releases from RPEL → MRTF-A binds chromatin. FHOD3 is perinuclear (outer nuclear membrane), not nuclear interior.
structural_evidence: Mouilleron et al. EMBO J 2008 PMC2583105 — RPEL-actin assembly incompatible with F-actin geometry
Mouilleron et al. Science Signaling 2011 — pentavalent G-actin•RPEL complex distinctively different from F-actin
Nat Commun 2021 PMC8613289 — nuclear G-actin depletion promotes MRTF activation (not F-actin tethering)
fabricated_properties: FHOD3 as nuclear interior formin (it is perinuclear/LINC-tethered)

logic.finding: LOGICAL CONFLATION — two distinct mechanisms (RPEL-G-actin sensing and nuclear F-actin formation) incorrectly merged into fabricated RPEL-F-actin tethering

falsifiability.finding: WOUNDED — proposed experiments (SMIFH2, LatA) would yield results interpretable under the correct mechanism (G-actin depletion), making the proposed wrong mechanism unfalsifiable by its own tests

counter_evidence.finding: DIRECT COUNTER-EVIDENCE — established mechanism is G-actin depletion, not F-actin tethering. Nat Commun 2021: 'Nuclear-capture of endosomes depletes nuclear G-actin to promote SRF/MRTF activation.' MRTF chromatin binding requires G-actin-free RPEL, not F-actin-bound RPEL.
search_queries: RPEL domain G-actin F-actin binding specificity MRTF structure "does not bind" filamentous
MRTF nuclear retention mechanism G-actin nuclear export actin polymerization depletes monomeric
FHOD3 formin nuclear localization fibroblast actin polymerization
counter_refs: Mouilleron et al. EMBO J 2008 PMC2583105

grounded_claims: Nuclear actin polymerization under mechanical stimulation (Baarlink 2013 Science)
mDia1 formin promotes nuclear actin polymerization (Baarlink 2013)
MRTF-A RPEL domain binds G-actin (Miralles 2003 Cell)
fabricated_claims: RPEL domain contacts nuclear F-actin — STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE (EMBO J 2008, Sci Signal 2011)
FHOD3 as nuclear interior formin — INCORRECT (FHOD3 is perinuclear/LINC-tethered, Frontiers 2023)

hallucination_as_novelty.finding: CONFIRMED — hypothesis appears novel because RPEL-F-actin tethering has never been proposed. It has never been proposed because structural biology explicitly rules it out. The 'novelty' is an artifact of a fabricated protein property.

claim_level_verification.finding: TWO FABRICATED CLAIMS — (1) RPEL-F-actin binding (structurally impossible), (2) FHOD3 as nuclear interior formin (it is perinuclear). Grounds for KILL.
verified_claims: Baarlink 2013 Science — nuclear actin polymerization by formins
fabricated_claims: RPEL domain binding F-actin (structural studies explicitly rule this out)
FHOD3 as nuclear interior formin in fibroblasts (FHOD3 is outer nuclear membrane/LINC complex)

hypotheses.survival_note: Does not survive. Core mechanism built on fabricated protein property. Note to Generator: the CONCEPT of stiffness affecting MRTF-A residence time is worth exploring through the CORRECT mechanism (nuclear G-actin depletion → RPEL release → stable MRTF-A-SRF chromatin association).

critic_questions.question: What is the evidence for CaMKIIδ expression and HDAC4 protein levels in primary human lung fibroblasts? The CaMKII→HDAC4 pathway is characterized in cardiomyocytes — does the cell-type translation hold?

EP300 'freeing' by HDAC4/5 export is oversimplified. Class IIa HDACs repress via NCoR/SMRT/HDAC3 complexes. What is the specific mechanism by which HDAC4 export leads to EP300 recruitment at mechanoenhancers?

The 2024 Nat Comms paper (PMID 39578439) demonstrates lamin A/C tension → YAP threshold. How does C2-H2 advance beyond this published result? What specific prediction distinguishes C2-H2 from the known mechanism?

strongest_surviving_weakness.C2-H1: CaMKII→HDAC4→EP300 chain characterized in cardiomyocytes, not lung fibroblasts. Cell-type translation may fail.
strongest_surviving_weakness.C2-H2: Core concept already published in 2024 Nat Comms. Only mechanoenhancer H3K27ac extension is novel — arguably below discovery threshold.

retracted_paper_flag.title: Nuclear CaMKII enhances histone H3 phosphorylation and remodels chromatin during cardiac hypertrophy
retracted_paper_flag.impact_on_hypotheses: Cited in QG new_evidence list for C2-H1, but NOT in C2-H1 hypothesis itself. Does not invalidate C2-H1.
